questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that correctly expresses the following sentence in passive voice. The company will launch its new product in August.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Its new product will be launched by the company in August.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The new product will be launch by the company in August.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The new product will be launched by the company in August.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Its new product will be launch by the company in August.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option c. Rule: Active Voice ----- Passive Voice Subject + will/shall + Verb(1st) + Object ----- Object + will/shall + be + V3 + Subject Active Voice: The company will launch its new product in August. Passive Voice: The new product will be launched by the company in August. In the passive voice construction, "the new product" is used with the definite article "the" because it refers to a specific, identifiable product that has already been introduced or is known within the context of the sentence or conversation.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Words of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains spelling error. The atman is beyond the duality of good and bad, right and wrong, winning and losing. But the human mind and ego eculte when it can do something better than the other person or other team.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option c. The option that contains an error is: Eculte The correct spelling should be "exult." Exult: The correct term "exult" means to show or feel a lively or triumphant joy; to rejoice exceedingly; to be highly elated or jubilant. Duality: The quality or condition of being dual; the presence of two parts, often in opposition to each other, such as good and bad, or right and wrong. Beyond: At or to the further side of something; outside the limits or range of something. Atman: A Sanskrit word meaning the inner self or soul in Hindu philosophy. It represents the true self beyond identification with the physical and mental state.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Simpleton
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option c. The most appropriate synonym for the word "simpleton" is: Moron Simpleton: A person lacking in common sense or intelligence; a fool. Moron: An offensive term used to describe a person who is notably stupid or lacking in good judgment; a simpleton. Prodigy: A person, especially a young one, endowed with exceptional qualities or abilities; a young genius. Savant: A learned person, especially a distinguished scientist; a scholar. It can also refer to a person with a mental disability who exhibits extraordinary skills or brilliance in a particular area. Luminary: A person who inspires or influences others, especially one prominent in a particular sphere; a distinguished or eminent person.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order, except the first part. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. A. In school, you discuss topics and issues with your teachers and classmates; and after school you phone up, visit or play with your friends. B. Think about your day-to-day life and the various social interactions you have. In the morning, before going to school, you interact with your family members; C. but also facilitates your growth and development as an individual. D. In each of these instances, you are part of a group which not only provides you the needed support and comfort
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct sequence is indeed b: BADC B. Think about your day-to-day life and the various social interactions you have. In the morning, before going to school, you interact with your family members; A. In school, you discuss topics and issues with your teachers and classmates; and after school you phone up, visit or play with your friends; D. In each of these instances, you are part of a group which not only provides you the needed support and comfort; C. but also facilitates your growth and development as an individual.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option with the incorrect spelling word in the given sentence. Amidst rapid urbanisation and environmental challenges, the city is embrecing green initiatives such as rooftop gardens and vertical farming.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The option with the incorrect spelling in the given sentence is: Embrecing The correct spelling should be "embracing". Embracing: Accepting or supporting willingly and enthusiastically; holding closely in one's arms, especially as a sign of affection. Challenges: Difficult tasks or problems that require effort and determination to overcome. Initiatives: New plans or actions to solve a problem or improve a situation; the ability to assess and start things independently. Amidst: In the middle of; surrounded by; among.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. high on the hog
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Living in luxury and enjoying the best of everything
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Struggling to make ends meet
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Feeling sad and depressed
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Working hard without any rest
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate meaning is: “Living in luxury and enjoying the best of everything.”

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. P. This system, called exogamy (literally, marrying outside), meant that the lives of young girls and women belonging to families that claimed high status were often carefully regulated to ensure that they were married at the “right” time and to the “right” person. Q. They had no claims to the resources of the household. At the same time, marrying them into families outside the kin was considered desirable. R. This gave rise to the belief that kanyadana or the gift of a daughter in marriage was an important religious duty of the father. S. Sons were important for the continuity of the patrilineage, daughters were viewed rather differently within this framework.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct sequence is d: SQPR S. Sons were important for the continuity of the patrilineage, daughters were viewed rather differently within this framework. Q. They had no claims to the resources of the household. At the same time, marrying them into families outside the kin was considered desirable. This system, called exogamy (literally, marrying outside), meant that the lives of young girls and women belonging to families P. that claimed high status were often carefully regulated to ensure that they were married at the “right” time and to the “right” person. R. This gave rise to the belief that kanyadana or the gift of a daughter in marriage was an important religious duty of the father.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate word to fill in the blank. Despite his immense talent, he remained __________ throughout his career and never received the recognition he deserved.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option b: obscure Let’s see the meaning of options: The sentence suggests that despite having immense talent, the person did not receive recognition. The word that best fits this context is "obscure," which means not well-known or not recognized. Renowned: well-known and respected, which contradicts the idea that he did not receive recognition. Eminent: famous and respected within a particular sphere or profession, again contradicting the context of lacking recognition. Prominent: important, famous, or easily noticeable, which also does not fit with the idea of remaining unrecognized.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Choose the option that correctly converts the following direct speech into indirect speech. "We are going on vacation next week," they said.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} They said the we were going on vacation next week.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} They said that they were going on vacation next week.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} They said that we were going on vacation the following week.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} They said that they were going on vacation the following week.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct option that converts the direct speech into indirect speech is: They said that they were going on vacation the following week. Direct speech ---- Indirect speech Present continuous tense ---- past continuous tense

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate synonym for the underlined word in the following sentence. Vertical farming is another concept that fosters sustainability in Bangalore’s urban spaces.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option a. Foster: To encourage or promote the development, growth, or improvement of something; to nurture or support. Enhances: To improve or increase the quality, value, or effectiveness of something. Discourages: To dissuade or deter someone from doing something by instilling doubt or fear. Stifles: To suppress, restrain, or hold back something, often leading to a lack of growth or development. Hampers: To hinder, obstruct, or impede the progress or development of something, typically by creating obstacles or difficulties.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. The event will be canceled unless it will stop raining by noon.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} unless it stops raining
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} unless it would stop raining
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} unless it had stopped raining
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} unless it will have stopped raining
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct option to improve the underlined part of the given sentence is: unless it stops raining unless it stops raining: This option uses the simple present tense "stops raining," which is appropriate for describing a future condition. It means that the event will be canceled if the rain stops before noon. This option is clear and grammatically correct.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the word segment that substitutes (replaces) the underlined word segment correctly and completes the sentence meaningfully. She made few mistakes in her presentation, but overall it was excellent.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option c: "a few mistakes" The phrase "a few mistakes" is the appropriate choice here. "A few" indicates a small but significant number of mistakes, implying that there were some errors but not too many. This meaning fits well with the context of the sentence, which suggests that despite some errors, the presentation was overall excellent. Few: hardly any. A few: Some but not many. The few: not many but all available. Therefore, the correct sentence is: "She made a few mistakes in her presentation, but overall it was excellent."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Convivial
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate antonym of the given word "convivial" is: Reticent Convivial: Friendly, lively, and enjoyable, especially in the company of others. It describes an atmosphere or environment that is festive, sociable, and welcoming. Gregarious: Sociable, outgoing, and enjoying the company of others. It refers to a person who is fond of the company of others and tends to seek out social interactions. Reticent: Reserved, quiet, or hesitant to speak or express opinions. It describes someone who is reluctant to communicate or disclose information, often being shy or secretive. Genial: Warm, friendly, and kind-hearted. It refers to someone who is pleasant and affable in their manner, making others feel comfortable and at ease. Amiable: Friendly and agreeable in disposition; having a pleasant and easygoing nature. It describes someone who is kind and likable, often fostering positive interactions with others.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate idiom for the underlined segment in the following sentence. Brave people never hide a̲t̲ ̲t̲h̲e̲ ̲m̲o̲m̲e̲n̲t̲ ̲o̲f̲ ̲d̲e̲c̲i̲s̲i̲o̲n̲.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} When the crunch comes
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Turn back the hands of time
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} A hell of time
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate idiom for the underlined segment "at the moment of decision" in the given sentence is: When the crunch comes When the crunch comes: When a critical or difficult situation arises that requires immediate and decisive action. Beat the clock: To finish a task or reach a goal before a deadline or within a set time limit. Turn back the hands of time: To go back to the past or to reverse actions and events. A hell of a time: An extremely difficult or challenging period, or conversely, an extremely enjoyable time, depending on the context.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. To combat these problems, businesses are promoting initiatives like rooftop gardens and vertical farms.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate antonym of the underlined word "combat" in the given sentence is: Alliance Combat: to fight against or take action to reduce or prevent something undesirable. Alliance: to a union or association formed for mutual benefit, especially between countries or organizations. Skirmish: A minor fight or battle, especially one that occurs as part of a larger conflict. Warfare: Engagement in or the activities involved in war or conflict. Confrontation: A hostile or argumentative meeting or situation between opposing parties.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate word for the given phrase. Extremely fresh and clean as if new; in a very good condition
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate word for the given phrase "Extremely fresh and clean as if new; in a very good condition" is: Pristine Pristine: In a perfect, original, unspoiled, or untouched condition. Gloomy: unhappy and without hope. Lofty: Of impressive height, size, or degree; imposing or elevated. Plunge: To thrust or throw oneself into something, especially a fluid.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Billiards are a popular indoor game played in many countries around the world.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Billiards are a popular
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The segment that contains a grammatical error is: Billiards are a popular The correct form should be: Billiards is a popular The grammatical error lies in the subject-verb agreement. The word "billiards" is a singular noun that represents a particular game, even though it may seem plural due to its "-s" ending. Therefore, it requires a singular verb form. In the given sentence, "are" is a plural form of the verb "to be," but it is used incorrectly with the singular noun "billiards." The correct verb form to use with "billiards" is "is," which is singular. The corrected sentence would be: "Billiards is a popular indoor game played in many countries around the world."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. He has a passion for the photography and often spends hours capturing beautiful landscapes.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} He has a passion for the photography
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The segment that contains a grammatical error is: He has a passion for the photography "The photography" is incorrect because the definite article "the" is not needed before "photography" in this context. It should be "photography" without an article. The corrected sentence would be: "He has a passion for photography and often spends hours capturing beautiful landscapes."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Fill in the blank with the most appropriate option. The renowned artist's latest masterpiece was met with _______ acclaim from critics and art enthusiasts alike.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most appropriate option to fill in the blank is: unequivocal Unequivocal: Leaving no doubt or uncertainty; clear and unambiguous. Unanimous: Characterized by complete agreement or unity among all parties involved. Unqualified: Absolute; not limited or restricted by conditions or qualifications. Unanimous: Characterized by complete agreement or unity among all parties involved.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} From the options select the most suitable idiom for the sentence given below. "To follow a trend or popular opinion."
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Read between the lines
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Jump on the bandwagon
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Hit the nail on the head
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} Keep something at bay
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The most suitable idiom for the sentence "To follow a trend or popular opinion" is: Jump on the bandwagon Jump on the bandwagon: To join or support a trend, movement, or activity that is currently popular or successful. Read between the lines: To understand the hidden or implied meaning in a communication, often by interpreting what is not explicitly stated. Hit the nail on the head: To describe something accurately or precisely; to identify or address a problem or issue effectively. Keep something at bay: To prevent something unpleasant or dangerous from happening or from coming too close.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Unemployment arises from a variety of _________(1). One which is always recurring and the effects of which we have had a recent example, is the disorganisation of industry resulting from a long war; this is a serious _________ (2) admitting of no easy_________ (3) at the best of times. Again there is the unemployment which followed a marked diminution in the _________ (4) of any raw product, such as cotton; fewer hands are required in the mills and the factories. We may call this cause of bad harvests. Similar, but more serious is the effect of changes in industry due to the invention of machinery which does more work and require _________ (5) hands. Yet another cause is strike or lockout and this is more to be deplored because such a stoppage is due to a very trivial matter, perhaps the men are working half an hour longer than what their Union permits. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option b. The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1 is "causes." The passage would read: "Unemployment arises from a variety of causes. One which is always recurring and the effects of which we have had a recent example, is the disorganization of industry resulting from a long war; this is a serious problem admitting of no easy solution at the best of times."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Unemployment arises from a variety of _________(1). One which is always recurring and the effects of which we have had a recent example, is the disorganisation of industry resulting from a long war; this is a serious _________ (2) admitting of no easy_________ (3) at the best of times. Again there is the unemployment which followed a marked diminution in the _________ (4) of any raw product, such as cotton; fewer hands are required in the mills and the factories. We may call this cause of bad harvests. Similar, but more serious is the effect of changes in industry due to the invention of machinery which does more work and require _________ (5) hands. Yet another cause is strike or lockout and this is more to be deplored because such a stoppage is due to a very trivial matter, perhaps the men are working half an hour longer than what their Union permits. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option b. The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3 is "solution." In this context, the term "solution" is used to indicate a resolution or remedy to the serious problem of unemployment caused by the disorganization of industry during a long war. questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Unemployment arises from a variety of _________(1). Again there is the unemployment which followed a marked diminution in the _________ (4) of any raw product, such as cotton; fewer hands are required in the mills and the factories. Yet another cause is strike or lockout and this is more to be deplored because such a stoppage is due to a very trivial matter, perhaps the men are working half an hour longer than what their Union permits. The sentence explains that when there is a significant reduction in the quantity of raw materials (diminution), it leads to unemployment because fewer hands (workers) are needed in the mills and factories that process these raw materials.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Unemployment arises from a variety of _________(1). One which is always recurring and the effects of which we have had a recent example, is the disorganisation of industry resulting from a long war; this is a serious _________ (2) admitting of no easy_________ (3) at the best of times. Again there is the unemployment which followed a marked diminution in the _________ (4) of any raw product, such as cotton; fewer hands are required in the mills and the factories. We may call this cause of bad harvests. Similar, but more serious is the effect of changes in industry due to the invention of machinery which does more work and require _________ (5) hands. Yet another cause is strike or lockout and this is more to be deplored because such a stoppage is due to a very trivial matter, perhaps the men are working half an hour longer than what their Union permits. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} The correct answer is option a. The most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5 is "fewer." "requires fewer hands" - This is the key part of the sentence. It highlights that, due to the efficiency of machinery, fewer human workers (fewer hands) are needed in the industrial processes.
